items.title: Royal Marines board Russian shadow fleet oil tanker in English Channel
items.summary: Marines were joined by National Crime Agency officers in the six-hour long operation in the early hours of Sunday.

items.title: Swiss voters reject 10 million population cap, early projections say
items.summary: Not all votes have been counted, but the current trend suggests 55% of participants voted against the proposal.

items.title: Russian families use AI to 'resurrect' loved ones killed in Ukraine
items.summary: The highly controversial trend lies at the intersection of Russia's war on Ukraine, new AI technologies and grief.

items.title: Norway braces for verdict in rape trial of crown princess's son Høiby
items.summary: Marius Borg Høiby will appear via video link, almost three months after his trial came to an end on 40 charges, including four counts of rape.

items.title: The college scam that promised students fleeing war a new life in Finland
items.summary: BBC speaks to students from war-torn Myanmar who say they were cheated after paying to study abroad.

items.title: French town buries murdered child as questions mount over police failings
items.summary: The prime suspect in the killing of Lyhanna, 11, was reported to police nine months prior but never questioned.

items.title: Discovery of €1.2m jewellery prompts fresh probe into former Spanish PM
items.summary: José Luis Rodriguez Zapatero is suspected of being unable to show proof he paid import duties on the jewellery.

items.title: Sardinian beach bans umbrellas for 10 to 65-year-olds
items.summary: Visitor access restrictions at Punta Molentis come into effect after 2025 wildfires damaged coastal areas across southern Sardinia.

items.title: Dzeko's last dance could be Bosnia's new beginning
items.summary: As Edin Dzeko enters the final stage of his career, BBC Sport tells the story of an underappreciated player who survived war to make history for Bosnia-Herzegovina.

items.title: Pope Leo visits Canary Islands to highlight perilous journeys of migrants
items.summary: The Pope is appealing for a humane approach and respectful welcome for migrants seeking a better life.

items.title: Ryanair investigated over charging parents to sit with children
items.summary: The UK's competition regulator is investigating the airline over charges it imposes on parents to sit next to their child.

items.title: Sweden ditches plan to imprison 13-year-old serious offenders
items.summary: The Scandinavian country is currently grappling with children being recruited into violent gangs.

items.title: Fireworks illuminate Barcelona's Sagrada Família during Pope visit
items.summary: Pope Leo XIV says Barcelona's iconic Sagrada Família is a masterpiece of "stones, colours and light" during his visit to Spain.

items.title: Ukraine says missiles hit military plant deep inside Russia
items.summary: Ukraine's military also says it has struck a Russian oil refinery and a "shadow fleet" oil tanker in the Black Sea.

items.title: Zelensky under pressure to end row with Poland over WW2 name of army unit
items.summary: Poland is threatening to strip Ukraine's president of a state honour over the naming Ukrainian military unit after controversial World War Two fighters.

items.title: Rare footage captured of Great White shark in Mediterranean Sea
items.summary: A volunteer diver has described shaking as he filmed his encounter with an endangered Great White shark between Tunisia and Sicily.

items.title: 'No dead ends': What the Dutch can teach us about tackling youth unemployment
items.summary: The Netherlands has one of the world's lowest rates of 16 to 24-year-olds not in education, employment or training.

items.title: Armenia votes as Russia piles pressure on pro-West government
items.summary: Incumbent Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan is seeking a third term despite falling domestic support.

items.title: Steve Rosenberg: Russia's economic forum overshadowed by drone attacks on St Petersburg
items.summary: The BBC's Russia editor saw Putin's flagship economic event overshadowed by Ukrainian drones attacks.

items.title: Giant banquets rile radical left in France
items.summary: The feasting phenomenon run by Le Canon Français has become a hot political issue.

items.title: Fifa bans all water bottles from World Cup games
items.summary: Fifa has been warned it is putting fans' health at risk by introducing a bottle ban at this summer's World Cup.

items.title: Watch: Explosion at fireworks factory in Malta
items.summary: Two men were injured in the explosion and were taken to hospital with minor injuries.

items.title: Between celebration and confrontation: Paris after PSG victory
items.summary: Nearly 800 people were arrested after clashes with police following the Champions League final.
